name: ADULT RESPIRATORY DISTRESS SYNDROME
definition: SHOCK LUNG = POSTTRAUMATIC PULMONARY INSUFFICIENCY = HEMORRHAGIC LUNG SYNDROME RESPIRATOR LUNG = STIFF LUNG SYNDROME = PUMP LUNG = CONGESTIVE ATELECTASIS = severe unexpected life-threatening acute respiratory distress characterized by abrupt onset of interstitial edema with high protein content rapidly filling the alveolar spaces associated organization of fibrinous exudate + subsequent regeneration of alveolar lining + varying degrees of scarring
dominant_finding: interstitial edema (with high protein content) initially
distribution: perihilar areas of increased opacity following rapidly
discriminating_features: characteristic 12-hour delay between clinical onset of respiratory failure and CXR
interstitial edema (with high protein content) initially
perihilar areas of increased opacity following rapidly
widespread alveolar consolidation with predominantly peripheral cortical distribution

general: interstitial edema (with high protein content) initially
perihilar areas of increased opacity following rapidly
widespread alveolar consolidation with predominantly peripheral cortical distribution
gravitational gradient (best seen on CT) ← dependent atelectasis
inhomogeneous areas of patchy / diffuse ground-glass opacities
bronchial dilatation with improvement / even resolution
± progression to irreversible varicoid bronchial enlargement
CXR: characteristic 12-hour delay between clinical onset of respiratory failure and CXR
patchy ill-defined opacities throughout both lungs
massive airspace consolidation of both lungs
consolidation becomes inhomogeneous ← resolution of alveolar edema
reticular / bubbly lung pattern ← diffuse interstitial + airspace fibrosis
